meta.note: Conservative, well-documented edges only; each carries a source. Direction = provider -> consumer (supply_chain, customer) or owner -> owned (ownership); partnership is undirected. Many real links to non-EU/out-of-universe entities (TSMC, Microsoft, NVIDIA, SES, Hispasat) are omitted because the counterparty is not a node here.

types.supply_chain: source supplies a critical input to target (e.g. Zeiss SMT optics -> ASML)
types.ownership: source owns / controls (a stake in) target (e.g. Iliad -> Scaleway)
types.partnership: joint venture, consortium or strategic partnership (undirected)
types.customer: source provides product/service that target depends on (stack dependency)

relationships.note: Carl Zeiss SMT is the sole supplier of the projection optics at the heart of ASML's DUV and EUV/High-NA lithography scanners.
ASML holds a 24.9% minority stake in Carl Zeiss SMT (€1bn, 2016/17), deepening the EUV optics partnership.
Siltronic supplies hyperpure silicon wafers to major chipmakers under long-term contracts; Infineon is among named customers.
STMicroelectronics uses Soitec's FD-SOI / SOI engineered substrates; the two collaborate closely under France's IPCEI/France 2030 programmes.
Scaleway is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the iliad Group; its cloud/AI build-out is part of iliad's ~€3bn cloud & AI programme.
Orange relies on Ericsson for 5G RAN; Ericsson supplies roughly half of Orange France's RAN (Cloud/Open RAN evolution announced Nov 2025).
Orange relies on Nokia for 5G network equipment; the two extended their 5G deal targeting Cloud RAN.
Deutsche Telekom awarded Nokia a multi-vendor Open RAN contract for 3,000+ German sites (replacing Huawei).
Deutsche Telekom relies on Ericsson for RAN across much of its German network alongside the newer Nokia Open RAN deal.
Orange and Capgemini jointly own 'Bleu', a SecNumCloud-targeted sovereign 'cloud de confiance' for the French state and critical sectors.
STACKIT offers Aleph Alpha's PhariaAI on its sovereign cloud (Schwarz Digits TECH Conference, May 2025) — pairing German sovereign cloud with a German sovereign model provider.
STACKIT supports 'RISE with SAP' workloads on its sovereign cloud (announced May 2025).
ASML led Mistral AI's Sept-2025 Series C (~€1.3bn of €1.7bn) for an ~11% stake and a strategic-committee seat — anchoring a European AI/semiconductor value chain.
Mistral AI and Helsing partnered (Feb 2025) to build vision-language-action models for Helsing's defence platforms.
Saab took a ~5% stake in Helsing (€75m, 2023) and integrates Helsing's AI into systems such as the Arexis EW suite for the German Eurofighter.
Airbus and Helsing partnered (2024) to integrate Helsing's AI into the unmanned 'Wingman' loyal-wingman concept; joint AI dogfight trials in 2025.
Orange and Mistral AI partnered (Feb 2025) on European AI: network R&D and embedding Mistral models in Orange's sovereign generative-AI offerings.
Quandela and OVHcloud partnered (Nov 2025) to offer Quandela's photonic quantum processors on OVHcloud's European sovereign cloud from mid-2026.
Thales owns 67% of the Thales Alenia Space satellite joint venture.
Leonardo owns 33% of Thales Alenia Space.
IRIS²: Eutelsat is a core SpaceRISE operator and Thales Alenia Space a key industrial subcontractor building the EU secure-connectivity constellation.
IRIS²: Thales Alenia Space and OHB are both named industrial subcontractors in the SpaceRISE consortium.
IRIS²: Airbus Defence and Space is a core industrial subcontractor in the SpaceRISE consortium led by operators including Eutelsat.
IRIS²: Thales (Thales SIX) is a named industrial subcontractor alongside core operator Eutelsat on the EU concession.
IRIS² (indirect): Leonardo participates via Telespazio (Leonardo/Thales JV) and its 33% of Thales Alenia Space within the Eutelsat-anchored consortium.
Saab supplies the Arexis electronic-warfare sensor suite (~€549m of orders) to Airbus Defence and Space for German Eurofighters.
KNDS is a key customer for RENK transmissions (e.g. Leopard tank gearboxes); RENK calls KNDS one of its most important customers.
KNDS holds ~10% of RENK Group after trimming its post-IPO stake.
Airbus holds ~10.65% of Dassault Aviation; the two also partner on the Future Combat Air System (FCAS/SCAF).
Deutsche Telekom is among enterprise partners integrating Black Forest Labs' FLUX image models.
iliad founder Xavier Niel is among H Company's named backers (recurring anchor of the French sovereign-AI ecosystem).
planqc's QIAPO project (2026) collaborates with Infineon (with BMW and Saarland University) on quantum-related work.
Alice & Bob and Thales both participate in France's PROQCIMA national fault-tolerant-quantum programme.
Mistral AI uses Scaleway's (iliad) GPU sovereign-cloud infrastructure for training/serving.
OVHcloud offers sovereign-cloud deployment of Mistral models (reference architecture for Mistral Large) under EU jurisdiction.
Jenoptik supplies precision micro-optics/photonic subsystems used in semiconductor lithography toolchains (ASML/Zeiss ecosystem).
Safran and Airbus jointly own ArianeGroup (50/50), Europe's launcher prime (Ariane 6); also partners across aerospace programmes.
Airbus holds 37.5% of the pan-European missile JV MBDA (with BAE Systems 37.5% and Leonardo 25%).
Leonardo holds 25% of MBDA, Europe's primary complex-weapons house.
Exosens (Photonis) supplies image-intensifier tubes used in night-vision goggles built by Theon and other European integrators.
Theon International acquired a ~9.8% strategic stake in Exosens, linking the two European optronics/night-vision players.
Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace owns 49.9% of Finland's Patria (State of Finland holds 50.1%).
Telespazio is a Leonardo/Thales 'Space Alliance' JV owned 67% by Leonardo.
Thales holds 33% of the Telespazio JV (sister company to Thales Alenia Space).
Telespazio (services) and Thales Alenia Space (manufacturing) are the two halves of the Leonardo/Thales Space Alliance.
RFA is OHB's majority-held micro-launcher venture.
Airbus Defence & Space holds 15% of Hisdesat.
Indra holds 7.1% of Hisdesat directly and (via its ~89.7% Hispasat stake, which owns 43%) is moving toward effective control.
Avio's P120C/P160C solid boosters are used as strap-on stages on Ariane 6 (built by ArianeGroup, the Airbus/Safran JV).
SAP invested in n8n (May 2026, ~$5.2bn valuation) and embedded it as the agent-orchestration layer in SAP Joule Studio.
Iliad (Xavier Niel) is a founding backer of the Kyutai non-profit AI lab (~EUR300m 5-yr commitment with CMA CGM and Schmidt Sciences).
CANCOM integrated STACKIT's sovereign-cloud portfolio into its Cloud Marketplace (Nov 2025).
HarfangLab (EDR) and Sekoia.io (SOC/XDR) jointly build a sovereign 'European Defense Platform'.
